prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Lo, in this quarter most strange, the scribes of commerce have tallied their digital tributes, revealing a bounty of computational indulgences that doth exceed all prior auguries. Verily, the metrics of engagement—those cryptic runes they call "MAU" and "ARR"—have multiplied like rabbits in a monastic garden, confounding mine understanding with their arcane calculations. By Saint Spreadsheet's blessed ledger, the revenue streams flow not from fields or tolls, but from ethereal cloud-dwellings where programmers weave unseen contracts more complex than any feudal charter. Hearken, good lords and ladies, for these software mercenaries have conquered territories unknown, their conquests measured not in acres or bloodshed, but in something called "user retention" which, I swear by my quill, must be some manner of sorcerous enchantment.
